preview_text:
The model favors Mexico at 60% to win, a 47-percentage-point edge over South Korea's 13% chance. This asymmetry suggests a clear favorite, though the underdog retains meaningful probability of an upset.

The draw probability of 27% sits midway between the win chances, indicating competitive depth rather than a lopsided matchup. The 34-point spread between Mexico's win and South Korea's win probabilities reflects meaningful separation; a one-goal or two-goal Mexico victory would align with the model's distribution.

The sportsbook consensus aligns closely with the model on draw likelihood (both 27%) but shifts 8 percentage points from Mexico to South Korea compared to the model. Polymarket prices fall between the two sources, nearly matching the sportsbook line. The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading is more accurate; the divergence may reflect different risk assessments or edge opportunities worth monitoring.
